findings.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header is missing, which is a key defense-in-depth security header for restricting script and resource load sources.
findings.rawRequest:
GET / HTTP/1.1
Host: google.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AI-Detective/1.0 WAPT-Scanner
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8
Connection: close
findings.rawResponse:
HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently location: https://www.google.com/ content-type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 content-security-policy-report-only: object-src 'none';base-uri 'self';script-src 'nonce-Fr480fmm08YwzJV4gmx75g' 'strict-dynamic' 'report-sample' 'unsafe-eval' 'unsafe-inline' https: http:;report-uri https://csp.withgoogle.com/csp/gws/other-hp date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:31:12 GMT expires: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 09:31:12 GMT cache-control: public, max-age=2592000 server: gws content-length: 220 x-xss-protection: 0 x-frame-options: SAMEORIGIN alt-svc: h3=":443"; ma=2592000,h3-29=":443"; ma=2592000 connection: close 301 Moved

The document has moved here.
findings.reasoning: No CSP header found. In the absence of a CSP, standard HTML rendering browsers will load resources and scripts from any source, increasing risk if XSS exists. (Security Infrastructure Detected: Google Web Server)
findings.remediation: Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy header, setting default-src to self and whitelisting trusted hosts.

findings.title: Missing Strict-Transport-Security Header
findings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is missing, allowing browsers to request the site over plain HTTP in future sessions.

findings.detectionLogic: Verify presence of strict-transport-security header over HTTPS
findings.reasoning: Without HSTS, users are vulnerable to SSL stripping attacks where an active MITM attacker downgrades secure connections to insecure HTTP.
findings.remediation: Enable HSTS by adding the Strict-Transport-Security header (e.g. max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains).

findings.reasoning: Without nosniff, browsers may attempt to parse files (like images or user uploads) as JavaScript, presenting an XSS vector if file upload controls are weak.
findings.remediation: Configure your web server to return X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff for all HTTP responses.
